sources.finding: getOutputTimestamp correlates output contextTime with performanceTime; zero values mean no audio block has yet been processed, and currentTime must exceed output contextTime after rendering begins.
outputLatency is the appropriate latency estimate when exposed, but it is device-dependent and may change; the currentTime-contextTime difference is not a reliable substitute.

finding: A physical-device audio packet should preserve a monotonic getOutputTimestamp timeline. outputLatency must remain optional so Safari evidence can still record rendering without inventing acoustic timing.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

limits: Render-position coherence does not prove that a human heard sound.
estimatedAcousticTimeMs is unavailable when the user agent does not expose outputLatency.
The 250 ms performance-clock coherence bound is an operational capture guard, not a browser conformance threshold.
falsification_route: Reject the design if valid physical Safari captures routinely exceed the coherence bound despite a stable monotonic clock.
Reject any downstream interpretation that promotes render_timeline_observed to heard.
Revise the optional latency branch if a current primary implementation source demonstrates a different Safari exposure contract.
